Try TickLists free →

Your shopping list, always in the right order for any store

One master list, synced on everyone's device.
Need something? Tick it. Bought it? Untick it.

Every store remembers its own section order — no backtracking in the aisles.

Try TickLists free

Free to use · No credit card · Works on any device

Who it's for

Built for real life

ADHD & forgetfulness

Everything already there, sorted to your store's layout — before you even walk in.

Carers & elderly parents

Share your list with elderly parents — they tick & tag what they need, you can filter by the tag, and bag it all together at checkout.

Special diets & meal prep

Keto, gluten-free, batch-cook. Tag your plan items optionally with a colour code too, so you can see which foods to prioritise whilst shopping. Filter to just those foods too if you prefer.

Savvy shoppers

Tag items by which store has the offer this week. Shop across multiple stores without losing track.

Tradespeople & renovators

Long hardware lists with notes, sorted to each trade counter's layout. Never backtrack through a builder's merchant again.

Shared workplaces

One live list for the work kitchen. Everyone can tick whenever something runs out.

Other apps waste your time as you type in items, then delete them once bought or done.

Ticklists is different — keep one master list, tick when you need something, untick when it's bought. The list is always ready for the next shop.

Tick a Food item once. Appears in every Food store.

Tick "Apples" and it immediately shows as needed at every store you shop — each one already sorted to that store's own section order. No re-ticking per store. One tick does all of them.

🍎 Apples → ticked once
Aldi Dalston✓ Neededaisle 3 — Fruit & Veg
Aldi Bethnal Green✓ Neededaisle 1 — Fruit & Veg
Tesco Brixton✓ Neededaisle 5 — Fruit & Veg

The right items. The right aisle. Every time.

Demo coming — store switching
Watch the same list reorder for Aldi Dalston, then Aldi Bethnal Green — same chain, totally different aisle layout, sorted correctly every time

Why it's different

Built for how families actually shop

Tick what you need. Untick when it's bought.

Your items don't get deleted — they stay on your master list unless you edit them.

Running low on something? Tick it. In the store, click ‘Ticked only’ to focus ONLY on what you need to buy.

Once items are bought, bulk untick to reset the list and start over.

Every store in its own order

Your Aldi on the high street is laid out differently from the one close to your work — and both are different from your Tesco. TickLists maps each location individually. Set the section order once per store, and your list sorts itself correctly for wherever you're shopping today.

Aldi Dalston

Aldi Bethnal Green

Tesco Brixton

Same list — three stores — each in its own section order

Keep food separate from hardware

Ticking "Screws" in your Hardware list won't clutter your Food shop — and vice versa. Keep a list for Food (Aldi Dalston, Aldi Bethnal Green, Tesco Brixton) and a separate one for Hardware (B&Q Brixton, Wickes Camden). Each list knows its own stores.

Get set up in minutes, not hours

With bulk upload, just paste a list — one item per line. Add *Section names* at the top of each group and sections are created automatically. Your whole list, set up in seconds.

One list. Share and sync with everyone.

Mike at home uses up the last orange juice, ticks it on the list. Rachael leaves work, calls in to the supermarket on her way home — and arrives with orange juice and the rest of the shop.

Anyone can tick or untick. Every change shows the person's name and timestamp, so there's no doubling up, ever.

How it works

Four steps and you're shopping efficiently every time

Step 1

Build your master list

Add everything you ever buy — once. Paste in from a note or spreadsheet if you like — one bulk upload, not hours of typing and clicking save.

Step 2

In seconds, map each store once —

Drag departments into the right order for any store you shop at. Every location saved permanently — even two Aldis with different layouts.

Step 3

Tick what you need

Running low on something? Tick it. It shows as needed on everyone's phones — at every store — instantly.

Step 4

Shop, untick, tick, repeat

Brought the shopping home? Click 'Untick all' to clear the whole list and start again. It clears for everyone, with your name and time stamped on it.

Early feedback

"I tick what we're out of, and it's sorted in walking order, and live on my husband's phone within seconds. I didn't know I needed this."

— Early tester

The last shopping list you will ever need.

Always ready, shared and in sync. Free to use.

Repeating task lists with day-by-day filters, shared in real time. One tap to reset.

Your repeating tasks…
focussed to NOW

Create & share repeating task lists for work or home.

Filter to see only what you need to, tick as you go, and one tap resets it, ready to go again.

— works for days of the week, repeating shifts & flexible to any use.

Try TickLists free

Free to use · No credit card · Works on any device

Who it's for

Built for anything that needs to be done repeatedly

Habit builders

Tuesday yoga, Thursday physio — day tags mean only today's tasks show up.

Families

Share the school-morning routine between parents or nanny helpers. Make lunches, pack bag, do hair — see who's done what, every day.

Large households & properties

Water plants Thursdays, feed animals twice daily, clear gutters monthly — all in one shared place.

Business shift checklists

Opening procedure, shift handover, weekly Airbnb cleaning — same steps every time, easy to repeat and monitor.

Delegation & Responsibilities

Easily delegate tasks on a list, or whole lists to staff, family members or flatmates.

ADHD & Executive Function

No more forgetting steps. Build your routine once, and the list shows you exactly what's next — every time.

Not a to-do app. TickLists Routines are repeating task lists — the same steps that happen every day, every week, or every shift. A morning routine, a pre-print checklist, a weekly clean-down. Write it once and it's always there, ready to tick through.
Demo coming — day filter
Watch the routine switch from Monday's tasks to Wednesday's — only what's relevant today, nothing else

Why it works

Reliable routines

Only today's tasks, nothing else

Every task is tagged to the days it belongs to. On Wednesday you see Wednesday's list — Monday's gym session and Saturday's housework don't clutter your view. One tap on the day and you're focused.

Done. Reset. Ready again.

Tick off each task as you go. When you're finished, tap Reset Routine and it's clean and ready for next time — whether that's tomorrow morning, next Monday, or the next shift. Works for any schedule, not just Monday to Sunday, maybe you have a 10-day repeating shift pattern, it works for that too. Tap 'To do' to see only what's outstanding.

Before and after ticking — then tap Reset and it's ready again

Shared with your whole team

Everyone sees the same routine. When Mike ticks "Walk dog", Rachael can see it instantly — with his name and timestamp. No confusion about who's done what.

Colour-coded by tag

Each tag can have its own colour, so at a glance you can visually separate. If you choose to use days of the week for example, you can see which tasks are Mondays-only, which are every weekday, and which are weekend jobs.

How it works

Set up once, use every day

Step 1

Create your routine

Name it and add sections — Morning, Mid-morning, Lunchtime, Afternoon, Evening, whatever fits. One routine can cover your whole day. Collapse the sections to focus just on what's to be done now

Step 2

Use tags to organise, separate and filter

Add each task and tag it to the day or shift it applies — Each gets a colour. Tasks that don't apply can be hidden, so you only ever see what's relevant right now.

Step 3

Filter by tag, tick as you go

Tap the tag you want to filter the list. Work through your tasks and tick each one. Everyone on the list sees every tick in real time.

Step 4

Reset when you're done

Tap Reset Routine and it's clean and ready for next time — no leftover ticks, no clearing up. Works to any schedule.

Early feedback

"I set up our Saturday morning cleaning routine once, and now we both just work through it. When we're done, one tap resets it for next time. That's it."

— Early tester

The routine your household or team can use easily

Always ready. Shared in real time. Free to start.
